task: DEBUGGER CIRCUITSOLVER — analysis of connections, pinAssignments, component types, and edge cases

summary.overallStatus: HEALTHY — two non-blocking data inconsistencies found (both in simulable:false experiments)

connectionIssues.connection: connections array OK, but pinAssignments: led1:anode -> bat1:positive
connectionIssues.issue: pinAssignment target 'bat1:positive' is a battery pin, not a breadboard hole. This elettropongo/clay experiment places LED directly on battery terminals. The pinAssignment mechanism is designed for breadboard holes (format: bbId:holeId). Data inconsistency but NO runtime impact — experiment is simulable:false.

pinIssues.issue: pinAssignments map LED pins (anode/cathode) to battery terminal pins (bat1:positive, bat1:negative) instead of breadboard hole IDs. pinAssignments API contract requires targets to be breadboard holes. No crash because CircuitSolver only unions pairs where BOTH are in _pinRefs (line 812), and the connections array correctly describes the circuit topology.

falsePositivesDocumented.pattern: nano1:W_D* and nano1:W_A* pins (e.g. W_D13, W_D5, W_A0)
falsePositivesDocumented.explanation: W_DX and W_AX are VALID wing pins on the Arduino Nano R4 breakout adapter — physical pins on the opposite PCB edge. CircuitSolver handles them via wing pin aliasing (lines 774-806): each W_DX is auto-unioned with its DX counterpart. NanoR4Board.jsx defines all 17 wing pins with mapsTo references. These are not bugs.

falsePositivesDocumented.pattern: breadboard-full bus format: bus-plus-N, bus-minus-N (missing top/bot)
falsePositivesDocumented.explanation: breadboard-full uses bus-plus-N and bus-minus-N — CORRECT format per BreadboardFull.jsx lines 341-342. breadboard-half uses bus-top-plus-N / bus-bot-plus-N. They have different schemas. Both are valid.

falsePositivesDocumented.pattern: breadboard-full column numbers > 30 (e.g., a33, a50, f60)
falsePositivesDocumented.explanation: breadboard-full has ROWS = 63 (BreadboardFull.jsx line 15). Columns 1-63 are all valid. Holes a33, a50, f60 are within range for the full breadboard.

falsePositivesDocumented.pattern: lcd16x2 pins: rs, e, d4, d5, d6, d7
falsePositivesDocumented.explanation: These are VALID LCD16x2 pins defined in LCD16x2.jsx (lines 299-306). The component has: rs, e, d4, d5, d6, d7, vcc, gnd. Not bugs.

componentTypesValidation.notes: All 20 component types have _initState handlers. lcd16x2 falls through to default {} state (no dedicated _solveXXX) — acceptable since lcd16x2 only appears in AVR-mode experiments where CircuitSolver is bypassed by AVRBridge.

edgeCaseTests.name: EC-1.1 Short Circuit — solver does not throw on direct short
edgeCaseTests.details: battery+ directly to battery-; no exception

edgeCaseTests.name: EC-1.2 Short Circuit — getDiagnostics().shortCircuit = true
edgeCaseTests.details: _checkShortCircuit detects supply+ and supply- in same net

edgeCaseTests.name: EC-1.3 Short Circuit — onWarning callback fires
EC-1.4 Short Circuit — normal circuit NOT flagged
EC-2.1 Open Circuit — no throw when cathode floating
edgeCaseTests.details: LED anode connected, cathode disconnected; no exception

edgeCaseTests.name: EC-2.2 Open Circuit — LED.on = false
EC-2.3 Open Circuit — LED.current = 0
EC-2.4 Open Circuit — LED ON when closed (control)
EC-3.1 Reversed LED — solver does not throw
edgeCaseTests.details: Cathode at +, anode at GND; no exception

edgeCaseTests.name: EC-3.2 Reversed LED — LED.on = false
EC-3.3 Reversed LED — LED.brightness = 0
EC-3.4 Reversed LED — forward LED ON (control)
EC-4.1 Zero-ohm Resistor — no throw
edgeCaseTests.details: R=0 between battery poles; no crash

edgeCaseTests.name: EC-4.2 Zero-ohm Resistor — state defined after solve
EC-4.3 Zero-ohm Resistor with LED — no crash
EC-4.4 Zero-ohm Resistor — silent 470Ω fallback (KNOWN BUG KB-1)
edgeCaseTests.details: comp.value || 470: R=0 is falsy → 470Ω used. LED.on = true, LED.burned = false. Prevents infinite-current crash but masks student mistake.

edgeCaseTests.name: EC-4.5 Negative Resistor Value — no crash
edgeCaseTests.details: R=-100 also falls through to 470 fallback

edgeCaseTests.name: EC-5.1 Potentiometer 0% — resistance = 0
EC-5.2 Potentiometer 100% — resistance = maxR
EC-5.3 Potentiometer 50% default — resistance = maxR/2
EC-5.4 Potentiometer 0% in LED circuit — LED off
edgeCaseTests.details: Wiper at GND → 0V signal → LED dark

edgeCaseTests.name: EC-5.5 Potentiometer 100% in LED circuit — LED on
edgeCaseTests.details: Wiper at VCC → 9V signal → LED lit

edgeCaseTests.name: EC-5.6 Potentiometer clamped above 1.0
EC-5.7 Potentiometer clamped below 0.0
EC-5.8 Potentiometer resistance monotonically increasing

knownBugsDocumented.impact: Student who sets R=0 won't see overload warning. Circuit behaves as 470Ω. Prevents infinite-current crash but masks user error.
knownBugsDocumented.recommendation: Explicit check: if value === 0, use 0.001Ω (near-short) and emit onWarning('zero_resistor', ...) to alert student

knownBugsDocumented.description: Clay experiment pinAssignments use battery terminal refs instead of breadboard hole IDs
knownBugsDocumented.location: experiments-vol1.js: v1-cap13-esp1 pinAssignments: {led1:anode: bat1:positive, led1:cathode: bat1:negative}
knownBugsDocumented.impact: No runtime crash. Both experiments are simulable:false. Pure data contract violation.
knownBugsDocumented.recommendation: Omit pinAssignments for clay/direct-connect experiments, OR document battery-terminal refs as a valid special case in the pinAssignment schema

recommendations: CircuitSolver is robust across all 25 edge case scenarios tested
Two data inconsistencies found, both in simulable:false experiments — zero runtime impact
breadboard-half and breadboard-full use DIFFERENT bus naming schemas — document this in schema comments to prevent future mistakes
Wing pins W_D*/W_A* are first-class in CircuitSolver — include in any external pin validator or documentation
Consider adding explicit R=0 warning to help students understand the error (KB-1)
